original text:
The total market share of "non-store," or online U.S. retail sales was higher than general merchandise sales for the first time in history, according to a report from the Commerce Department this week.
"The days of the internet and online shopping being ‘just a fad' have come a long way over the years, but February's Retail Sales report (released Monday) highlighted another of many major milestones that the growth of online shopping has reached over the years," said Paul Hickey, co-founder or Bespoke Investment Group, which reported the statistic in a note to clients Tuesday.
The online sector, referred to as "clicks," has been slowly eating up market share in the past two decades. Its total rose from below 5 percent in the late 1990s to about 12 percent in 2019, according to the Commerce Department. In February, online sales narrowly beat general merchandise stores, including department stores, warehouse clubs and super-centers. Non-store retail sales last month accounted for 11.813 percent of the total, compared with 11.807 percent for general merchandise. To be sure, brick-and-mortar sales are higher when including other categories such as auto and restaurant sales.
"Sure, we had to go out to three decimal places, so the margin of different is extremely small, but looking at the chart the trend remains clear; the share of total sales for each sector are clearly going in opposite directions," Hickey said in a note.

original text:
While you may have seen the excitement of Bridal Fashion Week through Harness Magazine's social media, there's a lot of non-glamorous moments behind the scenes. While the who's who of fashion may receive the VIP treatment, most fashion week attendees are one among the crowd. We walk for miles rushing from show to show, stand for hours in heels and squeeze into tiny standing areas just for a glimpse of the gowns on the runway. From the outside, fashion week seems to be only glitz and glamour, but what remains untold is the hard work and long days that go into it all.
3:15 a.m.: I wake up to find my phone charger that's been hanging on by a thread finally died. I run to the 24-hour Walgreens down the street, still wearing my pajamas, to buy a new charger. Not an ideal situation the night before I'm supposed to do social media coverage of the shows. I put my portable charger in my bag before I hop back in bed, knowing I'll need it tomorrow.
9:30 a.m.: Waiting outside the Rime Arodaky show I bump into a friend who works in the bridal industry. She introduces me to her boss, who was a regular on "Say Yes to the Dress." I internally fangirl and text my mom to ask if this counts as a celebrity sighting. She confirms.
10 a.m.: The Rime Arodaky show is magical. Ballerinas dance down the runway between the ballgowns. The models sport wet hair that gives off an I just left dance class look while still being chic. I stand behind the back row and lean frantically over the woman in front of my trying to take unobstructed photos.
11:47 a.m.: I have time to kill between shows, so I head to the famous Chip NYC for a pick me up. I am revived by a giant cup of coffee and a chocolate chip cookie that I shamelessly Instagram.
1 p.m.: The next show on my schedule is Inbal Dror. I accidentally take the wrong elevator and pop out backstage. Startled, I'm quickly escorted out as I try to sneak a few backstage pictures. The show is filled with beachy gowns topped with oversized pearl headbands and tiaras perfect for a destination wedding.
3:24 p.m.: I get a standing ticket for the Gracy Accad show so I'm squished in a pack of bridal buyers, bloggers and press standing in the back of the venue. The show is running late so I stand for 45 minutes before it starts. I check my step tracker and realize I've walked almost 7 miles today. No wonder my feet hurt.
7 p.m.: I'm done with shows for the day and completely exhausted. I spend the rest of the evening writing notes and finalizing my schedule for the rest of the weekend while catching up on This Is Us.
9 a.m.: Bridal Fashion Week, unfortunately, falls on the same week as two big midterms. I head to class and grudgingly take my exams for the next few hours.
6:55 p.m.: Josh Hutcherson, who plays Peeta in "The Hunger Games," sits across from me on the subway on the way to my first event. We make eye contact for a second and my heart skips a beat. I become so distracted I miss my stop without even realizing. I end up getting off at the same stop as him and following him for a few blocks until I lose him on the busy street.
7:22 p.m.: I'm now late for the Ines di Santo x Baccarat party (worth it), but when I arrive it's incredible. Four elegantly dressed models sit perched in the store windows sipping champagne and waving at passers-by. There's endless champagne and a DJ dressed in a ballgown. For a few hours, I feel incredibly glamorous.
10 p.m.: I arrive home feeling like Cinderella after the ball. I spend the night with a pint of Ben and Jerry's and a rom-com.
9 a.m.: My first show of the day is a presentation from Chantal Romano, where each dress is inspired by a Disney princess. I spend the next hour playing Guess That Princess while nursing my hangover with a coffee.
11 a.m.: I arrive at my favorite show of the week, Galia Lahav. The setup is a white room with two backdrops in opposite corners. Each model spends about five minutes twirling and posing before she's whisked away and replaced by the next. I don't know what's better: the gowns or the floor to ceiling windows overlooking the Hudson River. They serve the most incredible mini quiches that have me glued to the appetizer table.
11:45 a.m.: I realize I stayed too long at the Galia Lahav show and have 15 minutes to make it to the show that's 30 minutes away. I forget that it's also Comic Con two blocks up and I'm suddenly in the middle of cosplayers and superhero costumes. The avenue I need to take is closed so I have to make a huge detour. Nervous I'm going to miss the show, I break out into a full run. Multiple Spidermen give me strange looks as I sprint down the packed streets in my leopard print dress and heels.
12:15 p.m.: I arrive out of breath at the Julie Vino show to find it is actually running late. I chug my complimentary glass of champagne and schlump into my seat as I realize I ran for no reason. The show's bohemian gowns and dreamy floral headpieces lift my spirits.
6:00 p.m.: I find my seat at the Jenny Yoo show. They show velvet bridesmaids dresses and lacy gowns I see every millennial bride wanting. After three days of nonstop bridal, my head is a jumble of tulle, beading and open back dresses. My brain switches into full wedding mode.
7:35 p.m.: I end Bridal Fashion Week how I wish I could end every week: cocktail party hopping. The Sarah Seven cocktail party is first on the list. The whole room is filled with floor to ceiling gold streamers. There's a live band, champagne and endless pizza. It's so beautiful I forget the quarter-sized blister that just made an appearance on my left foot.
8:15 p.m.: I make it to the end of the Sarah Nouri cocktail party. It's in the penthouse suite of The London and the view is incredible. I eat countless bite-sized desserts and sip rosé from a mini wine bottle. For a few moments, I bask in how glamorous fashion can be.

original text:
Ah Sam Florist, a local Bay Area California business offers floral and plant designs, decorative home and business décor, gift baskets of wine and gourmet items. From intimate or elaborate events and weddings, corporate events or moments of sympathy, Ah Sam delivers the finest nature has to offer. For over 81 years, Ah Sam Florist has been synonymous with offering the perfect selection of flowers and plant gifts from modern, contemporary, cutting-edge designs to traditional styles for the special moments and celebrations of life. Ah Sam's unique flowers and plants, seasonal varieties, and traditional favorites create the emotional connection of flowers with birthday wishes to celebrations of holidays to just because arrangements. From love and romance roses, anniversary, thank you, new baby flowers and gifts, get well flowers and sympathy, funeral flowers for the home or service, and much more enhancing lives with the beauty of flowers. We honor the celebration of the holidays with generations of our clients and welcome new visitors to our website with our seasonal and everyday collection. For the special occasions in life such as Valentine's Day, Mother's Day, Christmas, Hanukkah, New Years, Chinese New Year and more, please chose your hometown florist. We thank you for shopping a family owned local business!
Ah Sam Florist Delivery Service Ah Sam Florist offers same day delivery delivers to most San Francisco Bay Area and East Bay cities. We service all local hospitals, universities, funeral homes and memorial parks in the counties of San Mateo, San Francisco, Santa Clara, San Jose, Alameda, and Contra Costa. Or we'll gladly arrange out of the Bay Area, national or international delivery by a local florist through our network of preferred florists. Simply order online, call (650)341-5611 or visit us in San Mateo. Peninsula Bay Area Cities Atherton California 94027, Burlingame California 94402, Hillsborough California 94402, San Mateo California 94402, 94403, 94401, Menlo Park California 94025,Woodside California 94062, Belmont California, Brisbane, San Carlos California 94070, Redwood City California 94061 , Redwood Shores California 94065, Foster City California 94404, Palo Alto California 94306, 94301, San Bruno, Millbrae California 94030, South San Francisco California 94080, San Francisco, Half Moon Bay California 94019, Portola Valley California 94028, Los Altos California 94022, Pacifica California, Colma California 94080, Daly City California 94015. South Bay Area Cities Santa Clara California, Saratoga California, Sunnyvale California, Campbell California, Cupertino California, Los Gatos California, Monte Sereno California, San Jose California East Bay Cities Alameda, Alamo, Albany, Berkeley, Blackhawk, Castro Valley, Clayton, Concord, Danville, Diablo, Dublin, El Cerrito, El Sobrante, Emeryville, Fremont, Hayward, Kensington, Lafayette, Livermore, Milpitas, Montclair, Moraga, Newark, Oakland, Orinda, Pacheco, Piedmont, Pinole, Pleasant Hill, Pleasanton, Rheem, Richmond, San Leandro, San Lorenzo, San Pablo, San Ramon, Treasure Island, Union City, Walnut Creek. Hospitals: Peninsula Hospital in Burlingame, Kaiser Hospital, Mills-Peninsula Hospital in San Mateo, Sequoia Hospital, Stanford Hospital, Seaton Medical Center in Daly City.

original text:
These levers can be used with the Nexus 7-speed hub and the CJ-NX40 shift unit. The cable can be adjusted directly on the handlebars. The handy Revoshift rotary handle enables easy and precise shifting.
Have not used them right yet! But they seem to be good! Missing the older variants that acted as pressure controls!
Har inte hunnit använda dessa riktigt än ! Men de verkar vara bra ! Saknar de äldre varianterna som fungerade som tryckreglagen !
Fast delivery, excellent quality !!

Fast delivery, excellent quality !!

original text:
Yakut (or Sakha) is a Turkic language spoken in East Siberia. Since it has been on the periphery of the Turkic world for a long time, it has preserved a great number of archaic language traits. On the other hand, Yakut people had very intensive contacts to Mongols and Tunguses, as a result of which their language has changed significantly over the course of time. This dichotomy of ‘archaic' and ‘progressive' phenomena makes Yakut a very specific case not only for turcological but general linguistic investigations.
László Károly's study presents an elaborated analysis and description of the deverbal nominal derivational suffixes in Yakut as spoken in the pre-Soviet times. Based on the analysis, various aspects of Yakut and at the same time Turkic and Mongolic word formation processes are discussed, such as historical development of the suffixes from Old Turkic up to modern Yakut, changes in the system through language contacts, similarities to and divergency from the other Turkic languages. Besides a formal description, the functional side of the analysed derivational suffixes is presented in a systematic fashion, providing a general framework for future works on comparative derivational morphology.
processed_text:
Yakut (or Sakha) is a Turkic language spoken in East Siberia. Since it has been on the periphery of the Turkic world for a long time, it has preserved a great number of archaic language traits. On the other hand, Yakut people had very intensive contacts to Mongols and Tunguses, as a result of which their language has changed significantly over the course of time. This dichotomy of 'archaic' and 'progressive' phenomena makes Yakut a very specific case not only for turcological but general linguistic investigations.
László Károly's study presents an elaborated analysis and description of the deverbal nominal derivational suffixes in Yakut as spoken in the pre-Soviet times. Based on the analysis, various aspects of Yakut and at the same time Turkic and Mongolic word formation processes are discussed, such as historical development of the suffixes from Old Turkic up to modern Yakut, changes in the system through language contacts, similarities to and divergency from the other Turkic languages. Besides a formal description, the functional side of the analysed derivational suffixes is presented in a systematic fashion, providing a general framework for future works on comparative derivational morphology.

original text:
Ambassador Let's do it, Romania!
Organized by the Kiwanis Art Oradea and Communications Media, the event Trinket for Life aims to raise funds for the young Ana Maria, who fights against a brain cancer tumor.
The Romanian Association Vatra romaneasca held the Symposium dedicated Brancoveanu martyrdom - sacrifice for faith and nation.
On the occasion of New Year's Eve Oana Lianu will perform an extraordinary concert at the Opera House in Craiova.
Between December 17 to 21 the artist was in Germany, where she represented Romania at the International Conference of the Institute for Cultural Diplomacy, held this year in Berlin, along with other diplomatic envoys, distinguished political figures, cultural, and international academics.PhD. Oana Lianu lectured and performed two extraordinary concerts, on 18th and 21st.
Oana Lianu will attend on Friday night Red Cross Gala Romania, TVR 1 recording, media partner of the Romanian Red Cross.
Oana Lianu performs a great musical Intermezzo, singing and playing Romanian folklore live at TVR1, in the tv Romanian show Once in life, starting at 21. The artist performs together with the famous orchestra Lautarii from Chisinau.
Asociatia Pro Romania europeana organizeaza Simpozionul National dedicat Zilei de 1 Decembrie, in Sala Mare a Primariei din Oradea. Ansamblul folcloric al Facultatii de Arte a Universitatii din Oradea, dirijor si coordonator Conf.Univ.Dr.Oana Lianu va incheia acest eveniment printr-un concert de cantece patriotice.
Associate Professor Dr. Oana Lianu - Committee member of The Contest for vacant teaching positions published by the Ministry of Education in Official Gazette no. 277 of 19.07.2013, Part III.
The artist will perform an extraordinary concert on the occasion of Cultural Awards in Salaj county. The third edition of the gala is dedicated exclusively to Oana Lianu, during which she will award The Trophy of Excellence for her entire career.The event will take place in Zalau, Transylvania restaurant, at 18 pm.
Artista va sustine un recital deosebit in care va prezenta vocal si la 6 instrumente de suflat, intr-o maniera unica, Riturile de pasaj (Cantecul de leagan, Cantecul miresii, Cantecul de catanie si Bocetul), un schimb deosebit intre Facultatea de Litere si Facultatea de Arte.
The artist will perform an extraordinary concert at Gh.Lazar School from Zalau city.The occazion is the 35th anniversary and the launching of the book with the personalities who graduated here, among who is Oana Lianu herself too.
Oana Lianu is part of artists, very few in number, which combines exceptionally vocal with the instrumental interpretation. Pan flute and vocals give the feeling of a perfect symbiosis, forming a formidable duo ... It is noted the artist's personal way to play the greatest hits, by the harmonious interweaving of the pan flute with her voice - Autumn leaves, Ete Indien, Aux Champs Elysees, Besam mucho, Dream a Little Dream of Me, Summertime, The Skylark, etc. The addressed repertoire include an emotional universe, waiting and waiting, a universe in which the public is invited to enter unconditionally.
Spectacol de comedie bazat pe cupletele lui Constantin Tanase.Oana Lianu interpreteaza rolul Mariei Tanase, alaturi de actorul Sebastian Lupu, in rolul marelui comediant Constantin Tanase.
Concert cu prilejul implinirii a 115 ani de la nasterea doctorului Ioan Lianu, doctor in medicina, fondatorul Dispensarului si a Scolii din Dumbraveni, comuna Crasna, judetul Gorj, unchiul artistei din partea tatalui. Primăria Crasna i-a acordat Oana Lianu Diploma de Excelenţă cu ocazia acestui eveniment deosebit.
Clubul Escape găzduiește duminică 13 februarie, de la ora 20.00, un concert în primă audiție absolută susținut de prințesa naiului, Oana Lianu.

It is noted the artist's personal way to play the greatest hits, by the harmonious interweaving of the pan flute with her voice - Autumn leaves, Ete Indien, Aux Champs Elysees, Besam mucho, Dream a Little Dream of Me, Summertime, The Skylark, etc. The addressed repertoire include an emotional universe, waiting and waiting, a universe in which the public is invited to enter unconditionally.
Spectacol de comedie bazat pe cupletele lui Constantin Tanase.Oana Lianu interpreteaza rolul Mariei Tanase, alaturi de actorul Sebastian Lupu, in rolul marelui comediant Constantin Tanase.
Concert cu prilejul implinirii a 115 ani de la nasterea doctorului Ioan Lianu, doctor in medicina, fondatorul Dispensarului si a Scolii din Dumbraveni, comuna Crasna, judetul Gorj, unchiul artistei din partea tatalui. Primăria Crasna i-a acordat Oana Lianu Diploma de Excelenţă cu ocazia acestui eveniment deosebit.
Clubul Escape găzduiește duminică 13 februarie, de la ora 20.00, un concert în primă audiție absolută susținut de prințesa naiului, Oana Lianu.
